title: Kochia atriplicifolia
taxonomy: Kochia atriplicifolia belongs to the family Amaranthaceae and is classified as a species of flowering plant in the genus Kochia. It is native to Asia and is also known by its synonyms, including Bassia atriplicifolia and Kochia prostrata. The classification of Kochia atriplicifolia is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Caryophyllales. This plant is often found in dry regions and is known for its ability to thrive in arid environments. Kochia atriplicifolia is a versatile and adaptable species that can grow as a shrub or a herbaceous plant.

intro: Kochia atriplicifolia, also known as Chinese burning bush or Mexican fireweed, is a flowering plant native to Asia and North America, characterized by its vibrant purple or red flowers and potential medicinal properties.
